structured_payload_candidate.title: Zone Setpoint Overlap Prevention
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Software shall prevent heating setpoint from exceeding cooling setpoint minus 0.5°C (1°F), unoccupied heating setpoint from exceeding occupied heating setpoint, and unoccupied cooling setpoint from being less than occupied cooling setpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: When setpoints are configured or adjusted.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Enforce minimum difference of 0.5°C (1°F) between heating and cooling setpoints; ensure unoccupied heating setpoint ≤ occupied heating setpoint; ensure unoccupied cooling setpoint ≥ occupied cooling setpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The software shall prevent the following: a. The heating setpoint from exceeding the cooling setpoint minus 0.5°C (1°F) (i.e., the minimum difference between heating and cooling setpoints shall be 0.5°C [1°F]). b. The unoccupied heating setpoint from exceeding the occupied heating setpoint. c. The unoccupied cooling setpoint from being less than the occupied cooling setpoint.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Local Setpoint Adjustment Limits
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Generic Thermal Zones with local setpoint adjustment
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Local setpoint adjustment offsets shall be persistent software points, limited by absolute limits: active occupied cooling setpoint between 22°C (72°F) and 27°C (80°F), active occupied heating setpoint between 18°C (65°F) and 22°C (72°F).
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: When zone has a local setpoint adjustment knob/button.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Store offsets persistently; limit active occupied cooling setpoint to 22-27°C (72-80°F) and active occupied heating setpoint to 18-22°C (65-72°F).
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: As a default, the active occupied cooling setpoint shall be limited between 22°C (72°F) and 27°C (80°F). As a default, the active occupied heating setpoint shall be limited between 18°C (65°F) and 22°C (72°F).

structured_payload_candidate.title: Local Setpoint Adjustment Mode Restriction
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Generic Thermal Zones with local setpoint adjustment
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Local setpoint adjustment shall only affect occupied setpoints in Occupied Mode, Warmup Mode, and Cooldown Mode; no impact in other modes.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: When zone is in a mode other than Occupied, Warmup, or Cooldown.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Ignore local setpoint adjustment in unoccupied modes.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The adjustment shall only affect occupied setpoints in Occupied Mode, Warmup Mode, and Cooldown Mode and shall have no impact on setpoints in all other modes.

evidence_quote: The adjustment shall only affect occupied setpoints in Occupied Mode, Warmup Mode, and Cooldown Mode and shall have no impact on setpoints in all other modes.
evidence_text: The adjustment shall only affect occupied setpoints in Occupied Mode, Warmup Mode, and Cooldown Mode and shall have no impact on setpoints in all other modes.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Local Setpoint Adjustment Freeze During Demand Limiting
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Generic Thermal Zones with local setpoint adjustment
structured_payload_candidate.summary: At the onset of demand limiting, the local set-point adjustment value shall be frozen and further adjustment suspended for the duration of the demand-limit event.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Freeze the local setpoint adjustment value; ignore further local adjustments until demand-limit event ends.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: At the onset of demand limiting, the local set-point adjustment value shall be frozen. Further adjustment of the setpoint by local controls shall be suspended for the duration of the demand-limit event.

evidence_quote: At the onset of demand limiting, the local set-point adjustment value shall be frozen. Further adjustment of the setpoint by local controls shall be suspended for the duration of the demand-limit event.
evidence_text: At the onset of demand limiting, the local set-point adjustment value shall be frozen. Further adjustment of the setpoint by local controls shall be suspended for the duration of the demand-limit event.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling Demand Limit Setpoint Adjustment
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Active cooling setpoints for all zones shall be increased when a demand limit is imposed: Level 1: +0.5°C (1°F), Level 2: +1°C (2°F), Level 3: +2°C (4°F). Operator can exempt individual zones.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: When a demand limit is imposed on the associated Zone Group.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Increase active cooling setpoint by the specified amount for the demand-limit level. Changes are not cumulative.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The active cooling setpoints for all zones shall be increased when a demand limit is imposed on the associated Zone Group. ... a. At demand-limit Level 1, increase setpoint by 0.5°C (1°F). b. At demand-limit Level 2, increase setpoint by 1°C (2°F). c. At demand-limit Level 3, increase setpoint by 2°C (4°F).

evidence_quote: These levels are used at the zone level (see Sections 5.3.2.6  and 5.3.2.7 ) to shed demand.
anchor_repair_reason: evidence_quote replaced with verbatim section heading line
evidence_text: These levels are used at the zone level (see Sections 5.3.2.6  and 5.3.2.7 ) to shed demand.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Heating Demand Limit Setpoint Adjustment
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Active heating setpoints for all zones shall be decreased when a demand limit is imposed: Level 1: -0.5°C (1°F), Level 2: -1°C (2°F), Level 3: -2°C (4°F). Operator can exempt individual zones.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: When a demand limit is imposed on the associated Zone Group.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Decrease active heating setpoint by the specified amount for the demand-limit level. Changes are not cumulative.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The active heating setpoints for all zones shall be decreased when a demand limit is imposed on the associated Zone Group. ... a. At demand-limit Level 1, decrease setpoint by 0.5°C (1°F). b. At demand-limit Level 2, decrease setpoint by 1°C (2°F). c. At demand-limit Level 3, decrease setpoint by 2°C (4°F).

evidence_quote: These levels are used at the zone level (see Sections 5.3.2.6  and 5.3.2.7 ) to shed demand.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Window Switch Setpoint Override
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Generic Thermal Zones with operable window switches
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When window switch indicates window is open, heating setpoint temporarily set to 4°C (40°F) and cooling setpoint to 49°C (120°F). If window open during other than Occupied Mode, generate Level 4 alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Window switch indicates window is open.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Set heating setpoint to 4°C (40°F) and cooling setpoint to 49°C (120°F). Generate Level 4 alarm if window open during non-Occupied Mode.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: For zones that have operable windows with indicator switches, when the window switch indicates the window is open, the heating setpoint shall be temporarily set to 4°C (40°F) and the cooling setpoint shall be temporarily set to 49°C (120°F). When the window switch indicates that the window is open during other than Occupied Mode, a Level 4 alarm shall be generated.

evidence_quote: For zones that have operable windows with indicator switches, when the window switch indicates the window is open, the heating setpoint shall be temporarily set to 4°C (40°F) and the cooling setpoint shall be temporarily set to 49°C (120°F). When the window switch indicates that the window is open during other than Occupied Mode, a Level 4 alarm shall be generated.
evidence_text: For zones that have operable windows with indicator switches, when the window switch indicates the window is open, the heating setpoint shall be temporarily set to 4°C (40°F) and the cooling setpoint shall be temporarily set to 49°C (120°F). When the window switch indicates that the window is open during other than Occupied Mode, a Level 4 alarm shall be generated.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Occupancy Sensor Setpoint Adjustment
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Generic Thermal Zones with occupancy switch
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When space unpopulated for 5 minutes during Occupied Mode, decrease heating setpoint by 0.5°C (1°F) and increase cooling setpoint by 0.5°C (1°F). When populated for 1 minute, restore previous setpoints.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Occupancy switch indicates unpopulated for 5 continuous minutes during Occupied Mode.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Decrease active heating setpoint by 0.5°C (1°F) and increase cooling setpoint by 0.5°C (1°F). When populated for 1 minute, restore previous values.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: When the switch indicates that the space has been unpopulated for 5 minutes continuously during the Occupied Mode, the active heating setpoint shall be decreased by 0.5°C (1°F) and the cooling setpoint shall be increased by 0.5°C (1°F). ... When the switch indicates that the space has been populated for 1 minute continuously, the active heating and cooling setpoints shall be restored to their previous values.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Hierarchy of Set-Point Adjustments
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Setpoint adjustments shall be applied in order of priority: (a) setpoint overlap restriction, (b) absolute limits on local adjustment, (c) window switches, (d) demand limit, (e) scheduled setpoints based on Zone Group mode.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: When multiple setpoint adjustments are active.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Apply adjustments in the specified priority order.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The following adjustment restrictions shall prevail in order from highest to lowest priority: a. Setpoint overlap restriction (Section 5.3.2.3.a) b. Absolute limits on local setpoint adjustment (Section 5.3.2.5.b) c. Window switches d. Demand limit ... e. Scheduled setpoints based on Zone Group mode

structured_payload_candidate.title: Local Override Timer
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When thermostat override buttons are depressed, call for Occupied Mode operation shall be sent to the Zone Group control for 60 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Send Occupied Mode request to Zone Group control for 60 minutes.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Heating Loop Enable/Disable
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Heating Loop shall be enabled when space temperature is below heating setpoint and disabled when above heating setpoint and loop output is zero for 30 seconds.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Enable Heating Loop; disable when space temperature above setpoint and loop output zero for 30 seconds.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The Heating Loop shall be enabled whenever the space temperature is below the current zone heating set-point temperature and disabled when space temperature is above the current zone heating setpoint temperature and the loop output is zero for 30 seconds.

evidence_quote: The Heating Loop shall be enabled whenever the space temperature is below the current zone heating set-point temperature and disabled when space temperature is above the current zone heating setpoint temperature and the loop output is zero for 30 seconds.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling Loop Enable/Disable
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Cooling Loop shall be enabled when space temperature is above cooling setpoint and disabled when below cooling setpoint and loop output is zero for 30 seconds.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Enable Cooling Loop; disable when space temperature below setpoint and loop output zero for 30 seconds.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The Cooling Loop shall be enabled whenever the space temperature is above the current zone cooling set-point temperature and disabled when space temperature is below the current zone cooling set-point temperature and the loop output is zero for 30 seconds.

evidence_quote: The Cooling Loop shall be enabled whenever the space temperature is above the current zone cooling set-point temperature and disabled when space temperature is below the current zone cooling set-point temperature and the loop output is zero for 30 seconds.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling Loop Output Range
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Cooling Loop output shall be a software point ranging from 0% (no cooling) to 100% (full cooling).
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Output 0% to 100% representing cooling demand.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The Cooling Loop shall maintain the space temperature at the active cooling setpoint. The output of the loop shall be a software point ranging from 0% (no cooling) to 100% (full cooling).

evidence_quote: The Cooling Loop shall maintain the space temperature at the active cooling setpoint. The output of the loop shall be a software point ranging from 0% (no cooling) to 100% (full cooling).

structured_payload_candidate.title: Heating Loop Output Range
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Heating Loop output shall be a software point ranging from 0% (no heating) to 100% (full heating).
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Output 0% to 100% representing heating demand.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The Heating Loop shall maintain the space temperature at the active heating setpoint. The output of the loop shall be a software point ranging from 0% (no heating) to 100% (full heating).

evidence_quote: The Heating Loop shall maintain the space temperature at the active heating setpoint. The output of the loop shall be a software point ranging from 0% (no heating) to 100% (full heating).
evidence_text: The Heating Loop shall maintain the space temperature at the active heating setpoint. The output of the loop shall be a software point ranging from 0% (no heating) to 100% (full heating).

structured_payload_candidate.title: Control Loop Type Requirement
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Loops shall use proportional + integral logic; proportional-only is not acceptable. Integral gain shall be small relative to proportional gain. P and I gains shall be adjustable by operator.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Use PI control; allow operator adjustment of P and I gains.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Loops shall use proportional + integral logic or other technology with similar performance. Proportional-only control is not acceptable, although the integral gain shall be small relative to the proportional gain. P and I gains shall be adjustable by the operator.

evidence_quote: Loops shall use proportional + integral logic or other technology with similar performance. Proportional-only control is not acceptable, although the integral gain shall be small relative to the proportional gain. P and I gains shall be adjustable by the operator.
evidence_text: Loops shall use proportional + integral logic or other technology with similar performance. Proportional-only control is not acceptable, although the integral gain shall be small relative to the proportional gain. P and I gains shall be adjustable by the operator.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Zone State Determination
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Zone state is Heating when heating loop output nonzero and cooling loop output zero; Cooling when cooling loop output nonzero and heating loop output zero; Deadband when neither.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Determine zone state based on loop outputs.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Heating. When the output of the space Heating Loop is nonzero and the output of the Cooling Loop is equal to zero. Cooling. When the output of the space Cooling Loop is nonzero and the output of the Heating Loop is equal to zero. Deadband. When not in either heating or cooling.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Low Temperature Zone Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If zone is 2°C (3°F) below heating setpoint for 10 minutes, generate Level 4 alarm. If 3°C (5°F) below for 10 minutes, generate Level 3 alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone temperature below heating setpoint by threshold for 10 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Generate Level 4 alarm for 2°C (3°F) deviation; Level 3 alarm for 3°C (5°F) deviation.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Zone temperature below heating setpoint by threshold.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Low-temperature alarm: 1. If the zone is 2°C (3°F) below heating setpoint for 10 minutes, generate a Level 4 alarm. 2. If the zone is 3°C (5°F) below heating setpoint for 10 minutes, generate a Level 3 alarm.

evidence_quote: Low-temperature alarm: 1. If the zone is 2°C (3°F) below heating setpoint for 10 minutes, generate a Level 4 alarm. 2. If the zone is 3°C (5°F) below heating setpoint for 10 minutes, generate a Level 3 alarm.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Active Setpoint Selection by Zone Group Mode
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Active setpoints shall be occupied setpoints during occupied, warm-up, and cooldown modes; unoccupied setpoints during unoccupied, setback, and setup modes.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Use occupied setpoints in Occupied, Warm-up, Cooldown modes; use unoccupied setpoints in Unoccupied, Setback, Setup modes.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The active setpoints shall be determined by the operating mode of the Zone Group (see Section 5.4.6). a. The set points shall be the occupied set points during occupied mode, warm-up mode, and cooldown mode. b. The set points shall be the unoccupied set points during unoccupied mode, setback mode, and setup mode.

evidence_quote: The active setpoints shall be determined by the operating mode of the Zone Group (see Section 5.4.6). a. The set points shall be the unoccupied set points during unoccupied mode, setback mode, and setup mode.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Demand Limit Sliding Window Method
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Demand control function shall use a sliding window method selectable in increments of 1 minute up to 60 minutes, with a 15-minute default.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Use sliding window method for demand calculation.
configurable_values: Window duration: 1 to 60 minutes, default 15 minutes
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The demand control function shall use a sliding window method selectable in increments of 1 minute, up to 60 minutes, with a 15-minute default.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Demand Limit Level Reset Condition
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Demand limit level switch shall be set when measured demand exceeds limit; reset when demand is less than 10% below limit for minimum 15 minutes and time is no longer within on-peak/partial-peak window.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Set demand-limit level switch; reset when demand < 90% of limit for 15 minutes and outside peak window.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: When the measured demand exceeds the limit, the demand-limit level switch for that level shall be set; when demand is less than 10% below the limit for a minimum of 15 minutes, and the time is no longer within the on-peak or partial-peak window, the switch shall be reset.

evidence_quote: When the measured demand exceeds the limit, the demand-limit level switch for that level shall be set; when demand is less than 10% below the limit for a minimum of 15 minutes, and the time is no longer within the on-peak or partial-peak window, the switch shall be reset.
evidence_text: When the measured demand exceeds the limit, the demand-limit level switch for that level shall be set; when demand is less than 10% below the limit for a minimum of 15 minutes, and the time is no longer within the on-peak or partial-peak window, the switch shall be reset.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Single Setpoint Adjustment Moves Both Setpoints
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Generic Thermal Zones with single setpoint adjustment
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If zone thermostat provides only a single set-point adjustment, the adjustment shall move both active heating and cooling setpoints upward or downward by the same amount, within limits.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: When single setpoint adjustment is used.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Move both heating and cooling setpoints equally.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If zone thermostat provides only a single set-point adjustment, then the adjustment shall move both the active heating and cooling setpoints upward or downward by the same amount, within the limits described in Section 5.3.2.5.b.

evidence_quote: If zone thermostat provides only a single set-point adjustment, then the adjustment shall move both the active heating and cooling setpoints upward or downward by the same amount, within the limits described in Section 5.3.2.5.b.
